name: Matthew House Toronto — Emergency Shelter & Transitional Housing
description: Emergency shelter and transitional housing in Toronto for newly arrived refugee claimants, including specialized homes for youth and unaccompanied minors.
notes: Capacity 48 (reception house). No fee. Referral or self-referral accepted. Restricted to refugee claimants in Canada less than 28 days for emergency shelter; transitional program for vulnerable refugee claimants (adults, youth, children). Does NOT serve permanent residents. English services with interpretation referrals available.

name: Sojourn House — Emergency Shelter & Transitional Housing
description: Emergency shelter (94 single adults and 225 families nightly) and transitional housing (52 units, up to 2 years) for newly arrived refugees and refugee claimants in Toronto.
notes: Family referrals through Toronto Central Intake (416-338-4766); single persons may apply directly. Locations: 101 Ontario St (main shelter), 250 Queen St E (34 single men), 165 Grange Ave (families, approx. 225 persons). In-house health clinic for residents. Youth program for ages 16–24.

name: Romero House — Transitional Housing & Settlement Support
description: Transitional housing (19 units, up to 12 months) with wraparound settlement support for refugee claimants and undocumented people, plus a weekly food bank.
notes: Intake hours Mon–Wed 10 am–12:30 pm, 1:30–4 pm; Thu–Fri by appointment. Capacity up to 80 people. Walk-in program also open to refugee claimants not residing at Romero House for settlement referrals and housing search support. Serves refugee claimants, temporary residents, convention refugees, PRs, citizens, and undocumented persons. French available.

name: FCJ Refugee Centre — Transitional Housing (Women & Children)
description: Transitional housing for refugee claimant women and children (capacity 30, up to 12 months) paired with legal support, mental health referrals, and social services accompaniment.
notes: New client intake drop-in Mondays 9:30 am–4 pm at 208 Oakwood Ave, Toronto M6E 2V4. Also offers primary health care (no OHIP required), English language learning for people without status, food distribution, and legal/immigration case management. Languages: Arabic, English, French, Hindi, Portuguese, Serbian, Spanish, Tibetan, Urdu.

name: COSTI Immigrant Services — Housing Access Program
description: Housing workers assist individuals and families at risk of homelessness with finding affordable or subsidized housing, Toronto Community Housing advocacy, and access to the COSTI Rent Bank.
notes: Located at Sheridan Mall, 1700 Wilson Ave Suite 114, Toronto. Drop-in Mon–Fri 1–5 pm; Wed and Fri 9:30–11:30 am. Serves anyone in North York area facing cultural, linguistic, or socioeconomic barriers to housing, including immigrants and refugees. Languages: Albanian, Arabic, Farsi, French, Greek, Italian, Polish, Turkish, Russian, Spanish. Funded by City of Toronto and United Way.

name: WoodGreen Community Services — Housing Access Centre
description: Toronto's largest non-municipal affordable housing provider assists newcomers with affordable housing applications, waitlist navigation, and manages 30+ affordable housing sites across Toronto.
notes: Serves newcomers, seniors, youth, people with disabilities, and people experiencing homelessness, including immigrants, convention refugees, and permanent residents. Also offers settlement services including employment, childcare, and language training.

name: Toronto Rent Bank — Neighbourhood Information Post
description: One-time grants of up to $4,000 (or 3 months' rent) for Toronto residents who are behind on rent or need help with a first or last month's rent deposit.
notes: Eligibility: Toronto residents paying market rent with a source of income; NOT available to those receiving Ontario Works (OW) or ODSP; household income must be within threshold (e.g., max $46,500 for an individual). Must be on a lease or moving to Toronto. Applications accepted online or in person at NIP and multiple Local Access Centres including COSTI.

name: Ottawa Community Immigrant Services Organization (OCISO) — Housing Help
description: Ottawa-based settlement organization providing immigrants and refugees with housing information, referrals, legal aid connections, and comprehensive settlement counselling.
notes: Four Ottawa locations. Mon–Fri 9 am–5 pm by appointment. Serves all immigration statuses. Services in over 50 languages. Serves approximately 10,000 immigrants and refugees annually. Geographic focus: Ottawa.

name: Kinbrace Community Society — Transitional Housing
description: BC's longest-serving housing provider for refugee protection claimants, offering transitional housing for 3–6 months in East Vancouver with paired settlement support.
notes: Referral required through BC CHARMS system — no direct walk-in. Mailing address: PO Box 21732, 1424 Commercial Drive, Vancouver BC V5L 5G3. Settlement support includes accompaniment, school registration, legal information, and employment workshops.

name: ISSofBC Welcome Centre — Settlement Hub & Temporary Accommodation
description: Purpose-built settlement hub at 2610 Victoria Drive, Vancouver offering newcomer settlement, language, and employment services, with 17 temporary accommodation suites for government-assisted refugees.
notes: Temporary housing suites (up to 21 days) are for government-assisted refugees (GARs) only — not refugee claimants. Settlement and employment services are open to all newcomers including refugee claimants. For refugee claimant housing referrals, contact Inland Refugee Society at the same address: 778-328-8888. Co-located with VAST, Inland Refugee Society, and a medical clinic.

name: Inland Refugee Society of BC — Housing Search Assistance
description: Provides newly arrived refugee claimants with orientation, bus tickets, seasonal clothing, food vouchers, and referrals to refugee-specific or alternative housing in Vancouver.
notes: Located at ISSofBC Welcome Centre, 2610 Victoria Drive, Vancouver. Mon–Fri 9 am–4 pm. Manages a housing wait-list — not all claimants can access refugee-specific housing directly; organization also helps find alternative solutions.

name: S.U.C.C.E.S.S. — Settlement, Housing & Newcomer Services
description: One of BC's largest social service agencies providing free settlement, employment, language, housing support, and counselling to newcomers, and operating BC Housing–funded subsidized buildings.
notes: Head office: 28 West Pender St, Vancouver V6B 1R6. Subsidized housing buildings accessed via BC Housing Registry for low-income individuals, couples, families, and seniors. Settlement and housing support services open to refugee claimants and newcomers of all statuses. Languages: Cantonese, Mandarin, Punjabi, and more.

name: DIVERSEcity Community Resources Society — Newcomer Housing & Settlement
description: Multilingual settlement organization providing housing referrals, information on the Canadian housing market, and the BC Safe Haven program for refugee claimants in Surrey and surrounding areas.
notes: Address: 13455 76th Avenue, Surrey BC V3W 2W3. Mon–Fri 8:30 am–4:30 pm. Serves Surrey, Delta, Langley, and White Rock. BC Safe Haven program is specifically for refugee claimants. Staff speak Arabic, Korean, Mandarin, Karen, Spanish, Swahili, and Punjabi.

name: Legal Aid Ontario — Refugee Law Office
description: Free legal representation for eligible refugee claimants including Basis of Claim preparation, IRB hearings, Refugee Appeal Division appeals, Federal Court reviews, and Pre-Removal Risk Assessments.
notes: Toll-free: 1-800-668-8258. Address: 20 Dundas St West, Suite 201, Toronto M5G 2C2. Reception Mon–Fri 9:30 am–4 pm (appointment required before visiting). LAO certificate required — financial eligibility must be met. Does NOT assist with PR applications, sponsorships, or work/study permit renewals. Regional offices: Hamilton (905-528-0134), Ottawa (613-569-7448). French available.

name: Legal Aid Ontario — Client Services Line
description: Province-wide intake line providing legal information and referrals in over 200 languages, and guidance on obtaining a legal aid certificate for refugee and immigration matters.
notes: Mon–Fri 8 am–5 pm. Services in 200+ languages via interpreters. For immigration and refugee matters, callers are directed to the Refugee Law Office. Also provides duty counsel at courthouses. Information is available to all; financial eligibility applies for legal representation.

name: FCJ Refugee Centre — Immigration Legal Support
description: Free legal support and case management for refugee claimants and non-status persons including refugee hearing orientation, appeal information sessions, and accompaniment to appointments.
notes: Address: 208 Oakwood Ave, Toronto M6E 2V4. Walk-in intake Mondays 9:30 am–4 pm. Mon–Fri 9:30 am–5 pm. Also serves non-status and precarious migrants. No LAO certificate required for basic services. Languages: Arabic, English, French, Hindi, Portuguese, Serbian, Spanish, Tibetan, Urdu.

name: Chinese and Southeast Asian Legal Clinic (CSALC)
description: Free community legal clinic serving low-income Chinese, Vietnamese, Cambodian, and Laotian communities across Ontario on immigration, tenant rights, social assistance, and employment law.
notes: Toll-free: 1-844-971-9674. Address: 123 Edward St, Suite 505, Toronto M5G 1E2. Drop-in Wednesdays 1–5 pm; appointments other days. Languages: Cantonese, English, Mandarin, Vietnamese. Funded by Legal Aid Ontario. Most refugee cases referred to the LAO Refugee Law Office.

name: South Asian Legal Clinic of Ontario (SALCO)
description: Free legal advice and representation for low-income South Asians across Ontario on immigration, sponsorship, employment law, social assistance, gender-based violence, and tenant issues.
notes: Address: 45 Sheppard Ave E, Suite 106A, Toronto M2N 5W9. Mon–Fri 9:30 am–5 pm (Fri phone only). Walk-in Mon–Thu. Languages: English, Hindi, Punjabi, Tamil, Urdu, and other South Asian languages via interpreters. Funded by Legal Aid Ontario.

name: Centre for Spanish-Speaking Peoples — Legal Clinic
description: Free legal services in immigration, housing, employment, and social assistance for Spanish-speaking Ontario residents, provided regardless of immigration status.
notes: Address: 2141 Jane St, 2nd Floor, Toronto M3M 1A2. Appointments only, Mon–Fri 9 am–5 pm. Languages: English and Spanish. Service provided regardless of immigration status. Refers refugee law matters to Legal Aid Ontario. Funded by LAO.

name: Black Legal Action Centre (BLAC)
description: Free legal services for low-income Black Ontario residents who have experienced anti-Black racism, including immigration-related legal issues and representation.
notes: Toll-free: 1-877-736-9406. TTY: 1-800-855-0511. Toronto: 180 Dundas St W, Suite 1509 M5G 1Z8; Ottawa: 1 Nicholas St, Suite 402. Mon–Fri 9 am–5 pm. No walk-ins — intake by online form submission only. Funded by Legal Aid Ontario.

name: Legal Aid BC (Legal Services Society) — Immigration & Refugee Line
description: Free legal information, advice, and representation province-wide for low-income British Columbians facing refugee claims, detention reviews, deportation matters, and IRB hearings.
notes: Toll-free (outside Greater Vancouver): 1-888-601-6076. Address: 425–510 Burrard Street, Vancouver V6C 3A8. Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri 9 am–4 pm; Wed 9 am–2:30 pm. Financial eligibility applies. Interpreters available at no cost. Services in Cantonese, English, French, Mandarin, Punjabi, and Spanish.

name: Immigration and Refugee Legal Clinic (IRLC)
description: Free legal advice and representation for low-income people across BC with immigration or refugee legal matters not covered by the standard Legal Aid system.
notes: WhatsApp: 778-222-7568. Address: 810–510 Burrard St, Vancouver V6C 3A8. Mon–Fri 9 am–4:30 pm. Complete the online intake form before calling. Financial eligibility applies. Transitioned to a new host organization in February 2025.

name: Access Pro Bono Society of BC — Free Legal Advice Clinics
description: Matches individuals with volunteer lawyers for free 30-minute legal advice sessions at clinics across BC, with specialized programs for refugees and immigrants.
notes: Toll-free: 1-877-762-6664. Clinics available across BC. Specialized programs include a refugee program, residential tenancy program, and employment standards program. Low or modest income eligibility applies.

name: MOSAIC — Legal Advocacy Program
description: Helps immigrants and refugees in Vancouver and the Lower Mainland understand their Canadian legal rights and provides referrals to legal aid clinics, immigration lawyers, and other legal resources.
notes: MOSAIC is no longer accepting new direct immigration law clients as of current reporting — refers to IRLC (778-222-7568), Access Pro Bono (604-878-7400), Legal Aid BC (604-601-6076), and UBC LSLAP (604-822-5791). Serves all newcomers including immigrants, refugees, and temporary foreign workers.

name: S.U.C.C.E.S.S. — UBC Law Students Legal Advice Clinics
description: Periodic free legal advice clinics hosted at S.U.C.C.E.S.S. offices, staffed by UBC law students supervised by lawyers, covering immigration, tenancy, employment, and other legal matters.
notes: Clinics offered periodically — contact S.U.C.C.E.S.S. for current schedule. For low-income individuals who do not qualify for Legal Aid BC. Legal topics include immigration, criminal, small claims, employment standards, residential tenancy, and consumer protection. Priority given to ISIP clients.

name: ACCES Employment — Sector-Specific Bridging Programs
description: Sector-specific bridging programs for internationally trained professionals in finance, engineering, tech, HR, supply chain, sales, and more at 7 GTA locations.
notes: Main office: 489 College St, Suite 100, Toronto M6G 1A5. Mon–Fri 8:30 am–5 pm. Free services. Some bridging programs require permanent residence, Canadian citizenship, or convention refugee/claimant work authorization — refugee claimants still awaiting a work permit may not qualify for all programs. Pre-arrival online program (Canadian Employment Connections) also available.

name: ACCES Employment — Employment Services
description: Core employment services including skills assessment, job search strategies, resume development, interview preparation, job matching, and employment retention support for newcomers across the GTA.
notes: Free services funded by Employment Ontario. 7 GTA locations: Toronto, Scarborough, Mississauga, Brampton, North York. Call to book an appointment with an Employment Consultant. Refugee claimants who have received a work permit are eligible.

name: JVS Toronto — Newcomer Employment Services
description: Customized employment, bridge training, and job search support for newcomers from 130+ countries, with sector-specific programs in IT, business, architecture, construction, and health.
notes: IRCC-funded. Adults 18+. Most programs require CLB 7+ English and eligibility to work in Canada (PR, citizen, convention refugee, or refugee claimant with a valid work permit). Pre-arrival online services available. Ontario Bridge Training Program (OBTP) available for regulated and non-regulated occupations. North York office: 1280 Finch Ave W, Suite 607.

name: YMCA of Greater Toronto — Newcomer Employment Services
description: Employment Ontario services tailored to newcomers including skills assessment, career planning, resume and interview support, job matching, and Better Jobs Ontario skills training funding.
notes: Multiple GTA locations including Etobicoke (1530 Albion Rd, 416-394-2900) and Scarborough Milner. Adults 18+ with post-secondary education from outside Canada. Must be eligible to work in Canada. Better Jobs Ontario requires unemployed 6+ months and low-income household. CLB 6+ required for bridge training programs.

name: Career Foundation — Newcomer Employment Services & Bridge Training
description: Government-funded career coaching, resume and interview support, job matching, and an Ontario Bridge Training Program for internationally trained professionals across the GTA and Hamilton.
notes: Free, government-funded. Hamilton office: 110 James St S, Suite 200 (905-667-0321). OBTP includes regulated occupation pathways. All newcomers and immigrants are eligible for core employment services; OBTP requires international post-secondary education and eligibility to work in Canada.

name: OCISO — Refugee and Immigrant Supports to Employment (RAISE)
description: Ottawa-based employment program providing newcomers with career mentoring, networking, job search workshops, and the Federal Internship for Newcomers program, supporting refugees and immigrants into employment.
notes: Geographic focus: Ottawa. Serves all immigration statuses. Programs include Ottawa Mentoring Collaborative, Federal Internship for Newcomers (FIN), and pre-arrival employment supports. Services in 50+ languages.

name: YWCA Hamilton — Newcomer Women's Employment Services
description: Employment readiness program for newcomer women, youth, and gender-diverse individuals in Hamilton, connecting clients with employment counsellors and local employers.
notes: Direct line: 365-324-0247. Address: 75 MacNab St S, Hamilton L8P 3C1. IRCC-funded settlement program. Serves newcomer women, youth, and gender-diverse individuals of all immigration statuses.

name: COSTI Immigrant Services — Settlement & Employment Programs
description: IRCC-funded settlement services including employment information, referrals, ESL instruction, job search support, credential assessment guidance, and community orientation for immigrants and refugees.
notes: Main office: 1710 Dufferin St, Toronto M6E 3P2. Settlement services at 760 College St, 2nd Fl (Mon/Wed 8:30 am–8:30 pm; Tue/Thu/Fri 8:30 am–4:30 pm; Sat 10 am–1 pm). Languages: 16+ including Arabic, Farsi, Italian, Portuguese, Russian, Somali, Spanish, Ukrainian. Some ESL programs also open to Canadian citizens and visitors.

name: MOSAIC — Employment Services for Newcomers
description: Comprehensive employment services for newcomers in Vancouver and the Lower Mainland including career counselling, resume support, job search strategies, interview preparation, and WorkBC employment services.
notes: Head office: 5575 Boundary Road, Vancouver V5R 2P9. Surrey centre: 7134 King George Blvd, Suite 200. Open to all unemployed BC residents legally eligible to work; specialized services for immigrants, refugees, and temporary foreign workers. 400+ staff, 40+ years experience. Services in 100+ languages.

name: MOSAIC — Refugee Employment Support Services (RESS)
description: Dedicated province-wide employment services for job-ready refugees including career counselling, labour market information, job search, interview prep, and job maintenance support, delivered remotely.
notes: Email: res@mosaicbc.org. Address: 315-7155 Kingsway, Burnaby BC V5E 0A6. Services delivered remotely by phone, email, and online. Eligibility: refugees who are PRs, valid work permit holders, or under CUAET. English CLB 5 minimum required. Refugee claimants still awaiting a work permit may not qualify. Funded by IRCC.

name: ISSofBC — Employment & Career Services
description: Free settlement and employment services for newcomers in BC including first-language employment support, Canadian labour market orientation, career planning, and job placement.
notes: Welcome Centre: 2610 Victoria Drive, Vancouver V5N 4L2. Mon–Fri 9 am–5 pm. Also serves Prince George and Squamish. Open to all newcomers including immigrants, refugees, temporary foreign workers, open work permit holders, and international students.

name: DIVERSEcity Community Resources Society — Newcomer Employment Services
description: Multilingual employment programs for newcomers in Surrey and surrounding areas including skills assessment, workforce integration, employer connections, and career services.
notes: Career services email: careerservices@dcrs.ca. Address: 13455 76th Avenue, Surrey BC V3W 2W3 (career services also at 1107-7330 137th St). Serves Surrey, Delta, Langley, and White Rock. Staff speak Arabic, Korean, Mandarin, Karen, Spanish, Swahili, and Punjabi. All newcomer statuses eligible.

name: S.U.C.C.E.S.S. — BC Newcomer Services Program (Employment)
description: Free employment services for temporary foreign workers, international students, and new Canadian citizens including resume help, labour market information, and employer networking events.
notes: Multiple service centre locations across Metro Vancouver, Surrey, Richmond, Tri-Cities, and Fort St. John. Primarily serves temporary residents (work/study permit holders), naturalized citizens, and certain individuals without valid permits. Does not primarily target refugee claimants — see MOSAIC RESS (bc-employment-002) for refugee-specific employment services.
